title: Demystifying the cost versus benefits of sparse LLM acceleration
authors: Abhimanyu R. Bambhaniya, Sheng-Chun Kao, Geonhwa Jeong, Suvinay Subrimaniam, Amir Yazdanbaksh, Tushar Krishna
abstract: The rapid growth in LLM model sizes has intensified the need for sparsification techniques to reduce computational and memory overheads. This presents a unique opportunity to design accelerator chips that can exploit sparsity to improve runtime efficiency. Supporting sparse execution in AI accelerators requires coordinated enhancements across three key subsystems: compute, memory, and interconnect. However, there is a lack of systematic methodologies that provide architectural insights for building efficient sparse accelerators tailored to diverse sparsity patterns and model architectures. In this work, we systematically analyze trade-offs between area, energy, and performance under quality constraints across a broad range of AI models. We quantify the architectural impact of enabling sparsity on key hardware subsystems, and characterize how design choices in compute, memory, and interconnect affect overall efficiency. Finally, we explore the design space of sparse accelerators and identify optimal configurations for accelerating modern LLM workloads under practical constraints.
title_zh: 揭示稀疏LLM加速的成本与收益
abstract_zh: LLM模型规模的快速增长加剧了对稀疏化技术的需求，以减少计算和内存开销。这为设计能够利用稀疏性来提高运行时效率的加速器芯片提供了独特的机会。在AI加速器中支持稀疏执行需要在计算、内存和互连三个关键子系统中进行协调增强。然而，目前缺乏系统的方法来提供构建高效稀疏加速器的架构见解，以适应多样化的稀疏模式和模型架构。在这项工作中，我们系统地分析了在质量约束下，面积、能量和性能之间的权衡，涵盖了广泛的AI模型。我们量化了在关键硬件子系统上启用稀疏性的架构影响，并描述了计算、内存和互连中的设计选择如何影响整体效率。最后，我们探索了稀疏加速器的设计空间，并在实际约束下识别出加速现代LLM工作负载的最佳配置。
